# Study brief — remote-work transitions

Semi-structured interview study (N=8) with knowledge workers who
transitioned from co-located to fully remote work in 2020–2022.
Interest: how the transition reshaped identity, spatial-temporal
boundaries, and invisible labour at home.

## Sampling

Convenience sampling via professional networks; saturation
expected around N=6–8 per Guest et al. 2006 estimates.

## Interview guide

Six open-ended prompts covering the transition narrative,
home/work boundary maintenance, technology mediation, gendered
labour reallocation, and felt sense of professional identity.

## Inputs

- inputs/transcripts/P01_transcript.txt through P08_transcript.txt
  (de-identified, IRB-approved)
- inputs/context/study_brief.md (this file)
- inputs/context/IRB_protocol_2023-04.pdf (consent + protocol)
